title: Commentary on Key Financial Performance – December 31, 2026
content:
Campus maintains a net worth ratio of 10.33%, an increase of 4.38 basis points from the prior period and 36.26 basis points year-over-year, while reporting a solvency ratio of 1.10.

The delinquency ratio stands at 0.34% of total loans, while net charge-offs stand at 0.22% of average loans.

ROAA stands at 0.73% and net interest margin at 3.35%, with a decrease in operating expenses to average assets by -6.98 basis points from the prior period. Net operating expenses to average assets decreased slightly, reflecting disciplined cost management that supports overall profitability within the broader capital context.

Loans represent 78.75% of shares. Loan balances increased by 5.47%, while shares and deposits grew at a rate of 4.99%. The core funding ratio stands at 24.68%.

Taken together, continued monitoring of asset quality will remain important to sustaining performance.
